steps.title: Close the dash appositive
steps.content: The opening dash introduces the appositive “a strong and flexible material,” which explains willow. The blank must close that interruption and let “to craft baskets” complete what Susan Billy uses willow for.

steps.title: Reject sentence breaks
steps.content: A and B use periods, leaving “to craft baskets…” as a fragment. C uses a semicolon before a phrase that is not an independent clause. A matching em dash closes the appositive cleanly. The answer is D.
